question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent long-lived gRPC streaming connections via subscribeTopic() to exhaust the bounded thread pool in SpringApplication, causing a network partition-like state where legitimate clients cannot establish new connections, resulting in full service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection default of 5 in NettyProperties by opening many connections each consuming all 5 slots with slow-reading subscribeTopic() streams, causing the Netty server to partition legitimate traffic outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with startTime set to the minimum allowed value (0 nanoseconds) to force the PollingTopicMessageRetriever to scan the entire topic message history, saturating the database connection pool and partitioning the DB from the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe() in RedisTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated Redis subscription failures by subscribing to a high-volume topic and then abruptly disconnecting, causing the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval) retry loop to accumulate unbounded retry tasks that partition the Redis connection from the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen() in SharedTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user deliberately consume messages slowly from a subscribeTopic() stream, causing the onBackpressureBuffer(maxBufferSize=16384, BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR) to overflow and terminate the shared Flux, which partitions all other subscribers sharing the same Redis channel from receiving messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages() in TopicMessageServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscribeTopic() request that repeatedly triggers the missingMessages() gap recovery path by subscribing during a simulated Redis partition, causing unbounded topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() calls that saturate the DB connection pool and partition the database from the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages() in TopicMessageServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user open many concurrent subscribeTopic() streams with no limit and no endTime, causing incomingMessages() to create infinite Flux.defer() chains that hold Schedulers.boundedElastic() threads indefinitely, partitioning the scheduler from processing new requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: pastEndTime() in TopicMessageServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with endTime set to null (no end time), causing pastEndTime() to return Flux.never() and keeping the stream alive indefinitely, which when done at scale partitions the bounded thread pool and causes a network partition outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve() in PollingTopicMessageRetriever] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1))) in PollingTopicMessageRetriever by subscribing during a DB outage, causing an infinite retry storm that holds Schedulers.boundedElastic() threads and partitions the scheduler from serving other requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSharedListener() in RedisTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ConcurrentHashMap topicMessages in RedisTopicListener by subscribing to many distinct topic IDs simultaneously, causing the map to grow unboundedly and exhaust heap memory, which partitions the JVM from processing network I/O?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic() in ConsensusController] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with a very large limit value (Long.MAX_VALUE) to cause the flux pipeline in TopicMessageServiceImpl to never complete, holding gRPC stream resources indefinitely and partitioning connection slots from legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: convertTimestamp() in ConsensusController] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime.seconds just below 9223372035L to produce a startTime near Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the polling loop to run indefinitely with no results and holding a Schedulers.boundedElastic() thread, contributing to a partition of the thread pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll() in SharedPollingTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user cause the shared poll Flux in SharedPollingTopicListener to stall by subscribing to a topic that generates no messages, causing the RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) loop to run forever and hold the shared Schedulers.boundedElastic() scheduler, partitioning it from other polling operations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen() in CompositeTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CompositeTopicListener.listen() does not enforce per-subscriber rate limits, allowing a single unprivileged user to open hundreds of subscriptions that collectively saturate the Schedulers.boundedElastic() pool and partition the service from the network?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic() in TopicMessageServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the safety check Mono.delay(Duration.ofSeconds(1)) in subscribeTopic() at scale by opening many subscriptions that stall, causing hundreds of concurrent missingMessages() gap recovery queries to fire simultaneously and partition the database from the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: topicExists() in TopicMessageServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user send rapid repeated subscribeTopic() requests for non-existent topic IDs with checkTopicExists=false, causing the service to create and immediately tear down Flux pipelines at high rate, exhausting reactive scheduler resources and partitioning the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onBackpressureBuffer() in SharedTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a high-throughput topic and then pause reading (TCP zero-window), causing the onBackpressureBuffer(16384, BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR) to fill and throw an error that terminates the shared Flux, partitioning all co-subscribers from the topic stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe() in RedisTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly subscribe and immediately cancel subscriptions to the same topic, causing the unsubscribe() method to remove the topic from topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap and forcing a new ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer to be created on each re-subscribe, exhausting Redis connections and partitioning Redis from the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve() in PollingTopicMessageRetriever] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout()=60s) in PollingTopicMessageRetriever by subscribing to a topic with a very old startTime, causing each poll to run for up to 60 seconds and holding a bounded elastic thread, which at scale partitions the thread pool from serving other requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll() in PollingTopicMessageRetriever] Can an unprivileged external user send a subscribeTopic() request with a startTime far in the past, causing poll() to repeatedly query with maxPageSize=1000 rows per page and numRepeats=Long.MAX_VALUE, generating unbounded DB load that partitions the database from the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isComplete() in TopicContext] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with endTime set to a value just slightly above startTime, causing isComplete() to return false for the full endTimeInterval=30s window and keeping the stream alive longer than expected, which at scale partitions connection resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages() in TopicMessageServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where numMissingMessages is computed as a very large value (by subscribing just before a large gap in sequence numbers), causing topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() to issue a query with a huge limit that saturates the DB and partitions it from the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSharedListener() in SharedPollingTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SharedPollingTopicListener.topicMessages is a single shared Flux for all subscribers, such that subscribing with a slow consumer causes the shared Flux to stall and partition all other subscribers from receiving messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen() in PollingTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user open many concurrent subscribeTopic() streams using the POLL listener type, each creating its own independent RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) polling loop, exhausting the Schedulers.boundedElastic() thread pool and partitioning the service from the database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic() in ConsensusController] Can an unprivileged external user send a malformed ConsensusTopicQuery with topicID missing (hasTopicID() = false), causing toFilter() to build a filter with a null topicId that propagates through the reactive pipeline and causes a NullPointerException that disrupts the shared reactive scheduler, contributing to a partition?
